pi_items.source: Verified by: (1) install_wireshark.sh script line 90-93 specifies this download path, (2) File successfully downloaded and verified as legitimate PCAP format (pcap capture file, microsecond ts, version 2.4), (3) Official Wireshark project repository confirms this file exists in their sample captures collection

Extracted using 'tshark -z follow,tcp,ascii,0' on telnet-cooked.pcap. Telnet stream shows 'login: ' prompt followed by username 'fake'. Verified with strings command: '.fake' extracted from binary PCAP. Fallback logic in setup_task.sh (lines 84-87) confirms this is the expected value for this standard Wireshark sample file.

Extracted using 'tshark -z follow,tcp,ascii,0' on telnet-cooked.pcap. Telnet stream shows 'Password: ' prompt followed by password 'user'. Verified with strings command: '1user' extracted from binary PCAP. Fallback logic in setup_task.sh (lines 84-87) confirms this is the expected value for this standard Wireshark sample file.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 11 and task.json description field. This is the correct path where the agent should write the extracted credentials.

Verified in setup_task.sh lines 22-24 where this directory is created with restricted permissions (700), and lines 91-93 where credentials are written to this file in the format 'username: <value>' and 'password: <value>'.

pi_items.metadata_value: Not explicitly stated, but implied to be real data
pi_items.verified_value: Real data from official Wireshark project sample captures repository
pi_items.source: File downloaded from official URLs: https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/telnet-cooked.pcap and mirrored at https://gitlab.com/wireshark/wireshark/-/wikis/uploads/db0c22aeab36db4a3213a8012a5b5b84/telnet-cooked.pcap. Install script (install_wireshark.sh lines 90-93) confirms these are the official source URLs for this sample capture file.

pi_items.verified_value: PCAP format: pcap capture file, microsecond ts (little-endian), version 2.4, Ethernet, capture length 1514 bytes. File size: 9244 bytes. MD5: 5600a1fbc4a5cf5da2c17181630fa612
pi_items.source: Verified using 'file' command on downloaded telnet-cooked.pcap and md5sum. The file contains a real Ethernet telnet session captured with proper PCAP headers and timestamps.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses telnet-cooked.pcap, an official Wireshark sample capture containing a real telnet session (not synthetic data). The session transmits credentials in cleartext: username 'fake' and password 'user'. The PCAP file is downloaded from the official Wireshark project repository (https://wiki.wireshark.org and https://gitlab.com/wireshark/wireshark). The file is a legitimate PCAP format capture (9244 bytes, version 2.4, microsecond timestamps). The task requires agents to open this file in Wireshark, extract the credentials using the Follow TCP Stream feature, and write them to /home/ga/Documents/telnet_report.txt with format 'username: <value>' and 'password: <value>' (both lowercase labels, single space after colon, no quotes).
